LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF RACHEL COOK'S MOTION TO RECLASSIFY CASE FROM LIMITED JURISDICTION TO UNLIMITED JURISDICTION IS DENIED. MS. COOK'S CALCULATION OF COMPENSATORY DAMAGES IS BASED ON THE PREMISE THAT HER DAMAGES CAN BE BASED ON ANY DAY SHE CHOOSES FOR THE VALUATION OF THE LTC THAT WAS ALLEGEDLY WRONGFULLY TAKEN FROM HER. THAT IS NOT THE LAW. MS. COOK'S COMPENSATORY DAMAGES MUST BE BASED ON THE VALUE OF THE LTC ON THE DAY SHE LOST THEM. USING THAT VALUATION DATE, IT IS A LEGAL CERTAINTY THAT MS. COOK'S COMPENSATORY DAMAGES WILL NOT EXCEED $25,000. MS. COOK HAS NOT SHOWN THAT SHE HAS ANY PROSPECT OF OBTAINING ANY PUNITIVE DAMAGES. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/HK
PANEL OF ARBITRATORS ASSIGNED. REJECTION DATE SEP-27-2018.

(Amended) Notice And Notice Of Motion To Reclassify Case, Supporting Memorandum, And Supporting Declaration Of Rachel Cook
Off Calendar Jul-25-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. Andrew Wellman of Wellman & Warren, Tel. No. 1-949-580-3737 appearing
for the Defendant by telephone appearance;
No other appearance on the matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Rachel Cook's motion to reclassify case from limited jurisdiction to unlimited jurisdiction is denied. Ms. Cook's calculation of compensatory damages is based on the premise that her damages can be based on any day she chooses for the valuation of the LTC that was allegedly wrongfully taken from her. That is not the law. Ms. Cook's compensatory damages must be based on the value of the LTC on the day she lost them. Using that valuation date, it is a legal certainty that Ms. Cook's compensatory damages will not exceed $25,000. Ms. Cook has not shown that she has any prospect of obtaining any punitive damages. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk

Motion And Notice Of Motion To Reclassify Case, Supporting Declaration Of Edward F. Sears, And Supporting Memorandum
